original_abstract: This paper examines the formal and technical conditions for utilizing calcareous fly ash as an active pozzolanic-hydraulic component in cements and various binding agents. The study is supported by experimental data obtained from hydraulic road binders and masonry cements formulated with calcareous fly ash sourced from the Bełchatów Power Station. To provide a comprehensive assessment, the researchers evaluated binders with varying proportions of calcareous fly ash, as well as blended systems incorporating ashes from different combustion technologies, including siliceous and fluidized-bed combustion fly ashes. The scope of the investigation focused on key normative properties required for certification and practical application. The findings demonstrate that calcareous fly ash from the Bełchatów Power Station is a highly effective constituent for producing binders and cements that fully comply with current engineering standards and technical approvals.
humanized_abstract: Formal and technical conditions for using calcareous fly ash as an active pozzolanic-hydraulic component in the composition of modern cements and binders were presented in the paper. The studies carried out on hydraulic road binders and masonry cements with calcareous fly ash from Bełchatów Power Plant are provided. The calcareous fly ash was used in different contents in binders as well as in composition of mixtures in which ashes from different sources were used, i.e. siliceous fly ash and fluidized bed combustion fly ash. The main normative properties regulated by the certification and use of the binders and concretes were taken into account in the study. The obtained results confirm suitability of calcareous fly ash from Bełchatów Power Plant for building materials production, enabling obtaining of binders and concretes that meet current construction materials design specifications as well as technical certificates applicable in the construction industry.
